text:
I cannot wait to see this movie in theaters with my family.

The idea of a family having magical powers is just so "magically" cool.

It also reminds me of the concept of a children's novel I am working on called Kyle and Marina (about a selkie brother and sister from Scotland who evacuate to New Jersey to live with their grandmother, and must learn to work together and love one another to save the world), in which every mythical water creature has a special power (or 2), and the idea of the family losing their magic powers and Mirabel having to save it makes me think of my idea of the Flying Dutchman (the main antagonist) trying to steal the powers from mythical water creatures, end life and take over the world, and Kyle and Marina, along with a lot of friends having to save everyone (though Unlike Mirabel, the selkie siblings do have their own powers (Kyle can magically summon Claymore swords and Marina can make anything appear with her lovely singing).

I hope at the end of the film, Mirabel will be rewarded with her own power when she saves her family and the magic  (maybe the power of salvation for all magic).

text:
Mirabel is the first female Disney protagonist to wear glasses.

The word encanto comes from Spanish and means charm, spell, or enchantment. It is both used as a way to tell a magic incantation and as an adjective for "sweetheart".


This movie and Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) marks the first time in Disney history that two human female protagonists from two different Disney Animated Canon films have first appeared within the same year.

Second computer-animated Disney film directed by Byron Howard and Jared Bush, after Zootopia (2016), five years prior.

The fourth Walt Disney Animation Studios film to explore Latin American culture, after Saludos Amigos (1942), The Three Caballeros (1944), and The Emperor's New Groove (2000)

There's no post-credit scene in this film.

The second Disney's animated film of 2020s to be produced in a 1.85:1 aspect ratio, after Luca (2021).
This film was the first Walt Disney Animation Studios film that used the new animation and rendering system named "Maestro" and "Hyperion".

With this film, Mirabel takes the 14th throne of the Disney Princesses and becomes the first South American princess.

The tenth Disney animated feature film to release on a Thanksgiving week, after Beauty and the Beast (1991), Aladdin (1992), Treasure Planet (2002), Bolt (2008), Tangled (2010), Frozen (2013), Moana (2016), and Ralph Breaks the Internet (2018).

John Leguizamo's first animated film outside of the Ice Age franchise since Titan A.E. (2000), twenty-one years prior.

This is one of very few Disney animated feature films released outside of the 20th century to contain musical elements, the others being The Princess and the Frog (2009), Tangled (2010), Winnie the Pooh (2011), Frozen (2013), Moana (2016), and Frozen II (2019).

Set in Colombia, the film contains several nods and themes of magical realism (a literary genre using magical elements in ordinary settings) typical of the works by Colombian writer Gabriel Garcia Marquez. An example is in the trailer, where the fairy dust sparks in the Disney logo is changed with yellow butterflies, a recurring theme in Marquez' novel One Hundred Years of Solitude.

text:
I've always have been a huge fan of Disney movies, but never felt totally identified with a character. I'm Colombian and this gives me goosebumps. I have 3 brothers and can totally relate to Mirabel's story of feeling like your the only one who hasn’t anything special. The emotion and feeling of this film cannot be described in words. And neither can be described how this movie and Disney have changed my life. Only by watching this trailer, I couldn’t stop smiling and my tears dropping all over my face. It's like watching all my life and all my family. 

I am and I will always be thankful for this. Thank you @Disney for showing me my real purpose in life: Being an Animator and changing lives as all the artists behind this movies.

text:
Has anyone noticed the potential color symbolism with the Family Madrigal? All of the family members wear a prime color, also depending on what side of the family they're on: Pepa's family wears all yellow, and Julieta & Augustine wear blue with Luisa in indigo. Plus Isabella and Dolores are the oldest child in their families and wear slightly different colors to contrast; purple and orange respectively. Even Abuela has red. The only color not present is green, which is what Bruno's color is. 

The single exception is Mirabel, who wears a dress with the full rainbow of colors. I'll let you conclude what that represents.
Mirabel's glasses are green tho, and they help her to see. What other character serves an important role to Mirabel finding her way? Bruno.

text:
Looks like another trash 3d animated film with no art style pretty much the very exact same style as all recent films, from Disney... guess they are too cheap to hire a real artist anymore. 

 No Hybrid form of 2D an 3D its completely 3D trash... no real attention to detail its just a copy and pasted computer generated film with the creepy life like style again... Overseas 2D animation is bigger than 3D or they make hybrids combining both forms of art into one film. 

Over here in America the film isnt tailored to anyone its just marketed to the masses as cheap as possible with entire 3D animation. 
 
Its awful what western society has done to animated films...

Not only does western society mainly view animated films as childish they dont even care anymore to put art into their films...  America barely even taps the market for animated films.
Its mostly overseas work that caters to the other demographics instead of just family and Children. 

Eventually Disney is going to have accept the fact 2D animation is not going anywhere and will have to start taking risks for fall out of the game...

Studio Ghibli is even back to the drawing board making a few 2D animated films mixed with 3D at the current moment, and they have zero plans to ever phase out 2D animation because its superior to 3D in every way.. the only thing 3D animation does better is make things cheaper and quicker to produce. 




And some ex Disney workers are making 2D film to showcase how its completely different from 3D animation. 
No one is even teaching 2D animation anymore here in America because they think some how 3D can completely replace it..  They could have never been more wrong...... it still has tons of advantages over 3D.  

The magic and fantasy of animated films is completely leaving western society and becoming a copy and paste carbon copy of each 3D animated film. 

They are trying too hard to be life like and not embracing the creative fantasy of drawing details and making things look more unique.  

Ill have to start looking  for more over seas animated films that actually have style and art in their production so I can show my kids what a real animated film looks like and not some copy and paste 3D production like this crap... Shame western media doesn't understand 2D's  value and youth sees it as old and  isn't properly educated on the matter of difference.  

Over seas its booming Japan being the King of Animated films they use 2D and 3D for everything they have more respect for their work and don't just chase the #'s  hopefully westerners make 2D come back or they will end up further behind in the industry of Animated productions relying more on foreign productions.

text: What does Encanto mean ??
author: @BYurick

text:
I'm having an idea of a possible film Walt Disney Animation Studios could do in the future: one set in Spain, where a teenage girl living in Barcelona becomes fascinated with Spanish history, and one day, upon ingressing the Sagrada Família, discovers a book and upon opening it, she gets transported to a dark fantasy world set during the Spanish Inquisition (á la the game "Blasphemous") and to find her way to return home, must fight all sorts of absolutely inhumane monstrosities that were once human, much like the Apostles from "Berserk".

And for the logo variants in the opening, the Disney logo would play normally albeit tinted in dark purple until the view pans down to reveal a vastly different environment (similar to the "Mulan (2020)" opening logo variant), that of the City of Barcelona in the year 2029 illuminated with neon lights and dropped against an extremely dark nighttime sky (akin to "Blade Runner" with various hints of the opening logo variant for the 2017 "Beauty and the Beast" remake), and as the animation follows, we see the usual Sleeping Beauty/Cinderella castle replaced by the Sagrada Família, with more fireworks launching into the sky than usual and, when the view reaches its usual position, the pixie dust arc is drawn like usual, but is wider and brighter, and the Disney text fades in, made of platinum, plated with several linings of gold, copper and silver and incrusted with several precious gems, giving the text an ethereal beauty and otherworldly shine. During all this animation, a flamenco rendition of the "When You Wish Upon a Star" logo's fanfare is heard.

Then, the Walt Disney Animation Studios logo starts normally, though it has an aged parchment-like texture, and as the logo continues playing exactly just like it normally does, we see Mickey Mouse donning a conquistador outfit as he rides a carabela ship, and while the logo proceeds to settle in its usual position, the background is brown and while the text draws itself, it's done in a way that resembles ink. And while Mickey whistles the same cheerful tune of the normal logo, the melody is played on a guitar as it accompanies the whistling.

Either way, this is only an idea, and if Disney were to work it out, it would be pretty interesting. Now, who might agree with this concept, now that "Encanto" is almost out?

Encanto in Spanish is Charm, magic or spell but twhen I saw the trailer I remembered the story my grandmom told me.

In Philippines encanto (enkanto) means spirits or guardians. The old houses have guardians guarding it that's why sometimes the houses are alive. Most enkantos are living in woods (like fairies) and guarding the nature, when you do bad things to nature they will punish you.

text:
Encanto might be promising. We first see a case where the main character isn't alienated because she's different or has no gift, instead she's loved by those around her and they try their best to make her feel better about the fact she doesn't have powers. Then it's the fact that the protagonist is realistic.

Story wise, it can go two ways. Either A, she actually doesn't have powers and she'll learn why(adoption being the most likely) or B, she does actually have one but the conditions to manifest said power is pretty unknown or already there just not obvious. If it's the A route, I hope they don't pull a Deus Ex Machina and give her a power that can just outright save the house. If it's the B route, I hope it isn't something left field and something we actually see throughout the movie.

I think the best part would actually be the House. The fact it is alive and reacting to the main character makes it seem like a lovable character and perhaps will make the fact the house is actually breaking down even more tragic.

text:
For those who wonder why madrigals have powers and why cerberus appears in the trailer, here is the answer:
- the film is inspired by the book "one hundred years of solitude" or "Cien Años de Soledad" , where a family the buendía, have magic and the place where the story takes place is in a town called Macondo, where supernatural events are presented 
- the three-headed dog is not cerberus, it is called Booboo and is part of Colombian mythology

text:
Everyone is invited to my country, has everything from beaches to mountains with more than 5.000 meters high 
Some interesting facts: in Colombia 87 languages are spoken, 
Colombia was discovered in 1499 by Europeans
The national animal of Colombia is the Andean condor
Colombia's national sport is el Tejo 
The national musical instrument of Colombia is el Cuatro 
The national flower of Colombia is the May flower or orchid cattleya trianae
The national dish or food of Colombia is the arepas
The National Bird of Colombia is the Andean condor
The National Tree of Colombia is the Quindío Wax Palm 
We do not have national fruit, because here you will find a great variety of fruits 

Cali-the salsa capital of the world
Medellin-the city of eternal spring and flowers
Bogota-where height can fatigue tourists, but then get used to it, it has the most visited museum in the world "el Museo del Oro"
Cartagena-The heroic, the old town with its colonial streets are spectacular and the views of San Felipe Castle are even more 😊

text:
this is a story about disability.

if this movie actually has the stones to *permanently* de-power everyone and force the family members to indefinitely confront life on the same level as the "un-gifted" protagonist, then i'll be pleasantly surprised.

but disney properties have a terrible reputation with how they represent disabled characters, especially in regard to curing them instead of teaching the characters (and audience) to make their life more accessible and be more respectful.

smart money is that by the end of the story protagonist finds her special magical power after all and everyone lives happily every after, and the moral about everyone having special value despite their abilities will be rendered completely moot.

text:
Let me make a prediction:
The "theme" so to speak will be how "You don't need magical powers to be special" and then by the end SIKE! It turns out she does have magical powers and she uses that magical power to save the house and she is the most special one of them all and everyone will stand up and clap and respect/adore her now... This of course renders the whole theme about not needing magical powers to be special completely moot.

EDIT: 
REVIEW: Ok I have now seen the movie and since I got so many replies on this comment I'll make a short no-spoiler review. It's basically a near perfect film, the music was good with a pleasant reoccurring rhythm, I'd recommend not listening to the songs before seeing the movie as they are "instrumental" in the story telling and character building. The characters were well written and although my precious prediction about the movie wasn't "completely" wrong the ending didn't undo the story or lessen the lesson at all. I'm quite happy that Disney tried some new ideas in storytelling, without using stupid things like a twist villain like they did with Frozen, they also skipped using a designated comic relief character/companion which is one of the things that usually bother me in Disney movies. I started feeling worried that she would get an animal sidekick but luckily that role is entirely occupied by the casa, which doesn't overstay its welcome. They did a good job utilizing all the characters and although I'd have loved to see more, maybe 20 minutes more, but I didn't really feel like there was anything missing and importantly the character I found was the most interesting ended up being well executed and had a good amount of screen-time. Just like a good meal shouldn't leave you unfulfilled, it did everything it needed. As an adult I enjoyed it quite a lot, and my 8yo niece liked it a lot as well. 10/10, I'm going to come back to watching this movie in the future I'm sure.

NEW PREDICTION: Yes, I have a new prediction. Because as every movie Disney have made within the past 35 years that have been good they will give it a sequel or tv-show usually not as good. The story in the sequel will revolve around how someone come to the Encanto from the outside world, of course it will be a young man (because OF COURSE the main character will need a love interest to have a conflict with). They will fight within the family again about weather or not they should open up to the outside or not and Maribel will end up leaving with the new person to see the outside. The new love interest will do something to make Maribel angry (like revealing the location of the town) and Maribel will go back to warn about it but. The super strong family member will be about to close off the path inside to the Encanto with her strength but is stopped by Mirabel and they finally make contact with the outside world and everyone is happy again. Though there won't really be any message like in the first movie, the sequel is made "just because" and many of the well written characters will be reduced to one dimensional caricatures of their original.

text: Okay I know this is gonna be a risky comment and I'm gonna get possible racist accusations but I have to say this. This isn't a complaint, per say, but...does anyone else notice Disneys shift to specifically "very culture heavy" films? Moana, Coco(which is very good! WATCH IT!), Luca, Raya The Last Dragon. Has anyone else noticed this? I'm not trying to make accusations of any kind here and I'm not saying this is necessarily a bad thing but its starting to feel like they're wanting to be part of that "all other cultures are best so lets pander" mindset a lot of other people have had recently online. Like the whole "america sucks so lets celebrate everyone else" thing. Again, I'm not trying to say we can't look at other cultures and make films culture heavy. I'm just saying, the frequency of it is getting...headscratching(?)

text: I feel like there's nothing really tying the story itself to being colombian, it seems like the plot could be layed over a family of any culture / setting, there's no reference to the history of colombia -- there's nothing in the plot that depends on the setting. It's not like Coco where the story is specifically with a mexican plot device - Dia de Muertos / Xantolo. Disney keeps churning out these pandering movies based on minority cultures in the west, like Raya the Last Dragon based on SE Asian culture -- but says nothing meaningful about the actual experiences of these people in our modern world. It's just a distasteful form of PC pandering that's meant for marketing

text: Disney is founded by nazis, and its content are tools of indoctrination -- to normalize witchcraft and to make magic seem hip and cool. If you are the parent of a child or more, it's your DUTY to know what your kids are watching. The minds of children are very malleable and without proper guidance from the parent, its roots will develop into wickedness of one type or the other. It's not just one thing -- not just a single cartoon -- that's responsible for this, but when you weigh in all other factors you have a recipe for a "failed" generation. If you think this is a load of nonsense, then humor yourself and look into this. You won't have to look far unless cognitive dissonance is your thing.

text: Everyone! In 2019, there was a show called the Dark Crystal: Age of Resistance. A prequel series based off of a 1982 movie called The Dark Crystal. A dark puppet series set in a world of Thra, where the Gelfling find out the truth of the Crystal and the Skeksis rulers and begin a rebellion. This show was everything, amazing fantasy, great worldbuilding, good score, great characters, etc. Then Netflix cancelled it. I know Covid's made things harder for companies but this show doesn't deserve the fate it got. Jim Henson would've been proud of this series. So tell everyone about this show, show this comment, anything. Let's convince Netflix to bring it back or sell it to a company who will. #ForThra #SaveDarkCrystal

text: I love how they’re setting up why the one to save the house *has* to be her. With all these magical people around the audience would question why they don’t solve the problem, but if their magic is tied to the house and the house is losing its magic then it makes sense. They’ve grown accustomed to their gifts; it’s a part of them at this point, so of course the one best suited to saving the house would be the one who isn’t having to readjust how they solve problems. Though, it seems like the other members of the house aren’t being written out of the story the moment their powers are failing, which is also good. I’m glad they didn’t take that narrative shortcut, because it just wouldn’t make sense. Being without superpowers doesn’t mean they’re completely powerless; it only means they’re hindered. I hope they develop the other characters as much as I think they will because there is some serious character growth potential in that scene where the now-powerless strong lady is holding on to our protagonist on the edge of that cliff. I would love to see the family’s struggles with suddenly being powerless/without the skills they’re so proud of, then see them come into their own, learning the very same message they’ve been trying to tell the protagonist: you don’t have to have powers to be special. I’d love to see a final showdown, not where our protagonist has to finish the fight alone, but instead where the family works together to help the protagonist achieve the end goal. They get their powers back, but they don’t care right away because look at what they accomplished! It was *difficult* and *challenging* and they *did it! Without their powers!!* They get their powers back and our protagonist stays powerless, but all of them come out with a greater sense of identity. This movie has the potential to be a masterpiece if done right

text:
Recipe for Encanto.
Start with Coco as the base.
Mix in 200ml of the 'everyone has cool powers but me' trope from My Hero Academia, Sky High etc.
Add 2 tablespoons of the 'Sentient magical house' trope from Howl's Moving Castle, Monster House, Doctor Strange etc. 
Crack in the "magic is leaving the world/someone is trying to steal the magic/a dark power is growing' plotline, from Moana, Raya, or your pick of Ghibli, Early Dreamworks or Star Wars movie. Stir til stiff peaks for.
Bake for 2 hours. 
Serve with a drizzle of Saccharine.

text:
This is so predictable I already know it’s gonna go 3 ways:
1. A villain/force wants to takes away the magic and everyone’s powers
2. She goes on a extremely long journey to “save” the magic
3. At the end she comes to the generic Disney conclusion she has no gift and her gift is bringing everyone together with friendship and love and all that shit
Or:
1. Someone from the family is secretly the villain and wants to take everyone’s gifts for themselves and is draining the magic or some plot twist villain, or a monster or force 
2. She adventures out to find a magical source/“the candle” that gave everyone the powers to save the magic
3. She find the candle and everything is sunshine and rainbows
And the 3rd way is:
1. Everyone ridicules her for being gift less, she seeks out help from the antagonist to get a gift
2. It backfires somehow and ends with the magic disappearing and being stolen
3. She goes to find new magic
4. Realises she doesn’t have a gift and her real magic is ThE pOwEr Of LoVe

text:
Please read this carefully ;

Every one of us has sinned against God. We’ve lied, we’ve stolen, we’ve sinned sexually, and we’ve taken His name in vain; which makes us liars, thieves, fornicators and blasphemers ... Yet this is only four of our many transgressions .

The problem is, we can never ‘outweigh’ our sin with good works because God is perfect in Holiness, meaning his standard is beyond our reach. He is also perfect in Justice, meaning, he will bring EVERY sin to light. The punishment for our sin is death, and Hell; a place of Eternal Fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth .

Fortunately God is also perfect in Love; so 2000 years ago, he left his throne in heaven, and came to earth as a man named Jesus Christ. While here, he lived a perfectly righteous life, only to take the punishment that We deserved; Our sinless creator became Our sin, and endured a gruesome death on a cross for our sake . 
Right before he died, he said ‘it is finished’ .. meaning, the debt for our sins has been paid in full. And on the third day, he defeated death and rose from the grave. So now, we can go free from the wrath of God’s judgement not by earning it, but rather, as a free gift of grace ..

Except gifts can be either accepted or rejected. So this is what you must do in response ; 

Humbly admit to God that you have sinned against him. Then, confess with your mouth that Jesus Christ is Lord, and your Savior. ( Romans 10:9 ) . 

Do this, and you will be saved .

If you do so sincerely, God will grant you a new heart which no longer finds comfort in sin, but instead desires to read and obey his word. He will also grant you Eternal life in his presence; The greatest pleasure on earth doesn’t even come close to what God is preparing for believers in the afterlife .

You’ve been informed ; Now the choice is yours. What you do with this information will determine where you will be for ETERNITY.. Meaning, this is the single most critical decision you will EVER make .

Jesus Christ awaits you this very moment. Do not put off surrendering your life to him, because your tomorrow isn’t guaranteed... and as it is written ( Hebrews 9 : 27 );

‘It is appointed unto man ONCE to die, but after this, THE JUDGMENT’.

Engkanto (from Spanish encanto) are mythical
environmental spirits that are said to have the
ability to appear in human form. They are often
associated with the spirits of ancestors in the
Philippines. They are also characterized as spirit
sorts like sirens, dark beings, elves, and more.

text:
Please read this carefully ;

Every one of us has sinned against God. We’ve lied, we’ve stolen, we’ve sinned sexually, and we’ve taken His name in vain; which makes us liars, thieves, fornicators and blasphemers ... Yet this is only four of our many transgressions .

The problem is, we can never ‘outweigh’ our sin with good works because God is perfect in Holiness, meaning his standard is beyond our reach. He is also perfect in Justice, meaning, he will bring EVERY sin to light. The punishment for our sin is death, and Hell; a place of Eternal Fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth .

Fortunately God is also perfect in Love; so 2000 years ago, he left his throne in heaven, and came to earth as a man named Jesus Christ. While here, he lived a perfectly righteous life, only to take the punishment that We deserved; Our sinless creator became Our sin, and endured a gruesome death on a cross for our sake . 
Right before he died, he said ‘it is finished’ .. meaning, the debt for our sins has been paid in full. And on the third day, he defeated death and rose from the grave. So now, we can go free from the wrath of God’s judgement not by earning it, but rather, as a free gift of grace ..

Except gifts can be either accepted or rejected. So this is what you must do in response ; 

Humbly admit to God that you have sinned against him. Then, confess with your mouth that Jesus Christ is Lord, and your Savior. ( Romans 10:9 ) . 

Do this, and you will be saved .

If you do so sincerely, God will grant you a new heart which no longer finds comfort in sin, but instead desires to read and obey his word. He will also grant you Eternal life in his presence; The greatest pleasure on earth doesn’t even come close to what God is preparing for believers in the afterlife .

You’ve been informed ; Now the choice is yours. What you do with this information will determine where you will be for ETERNITY.. Meaning, this is the single most critical decision you will EVER make .

Jesus Christ awaits you this very moment. Do not put off surrendering your life to him, because your tomorrow isn’t guaranteed... and as it is written ( Hebrews 9 : 27 );

‘It is appointed unto man ONCE to die, but after this, THE JUDGMENT’.

author: @Thefoxthatbecameawolf

text:
MY GOD!!! DISNEY, IS IT SOOO GOOD TO HAVE YOU BACK!!!! Hell even the quality of this movie look so d*mn good! Like holy crap! And I thought Raya and The Last Dragon would be the one to take over 2021 and to make this year all the more worth it. Like I said in my review of Raya, the original idea party from Disney doesn't stop there and this is a significant example of how the party doesn't stop with Raya and The Last Dragon! Nope, the party still continues with Encanto! And good freakin lord, where the hell do I start with this trailer? The energy and pacing seems to grab my attention at every single level especially when it represents the grand scale of Maribel's journey as well as figuring out what her powers would be, the animation looks a-freakin-mazing as always especially when the visual effects of their magic powers come into play which would bring in a lot of creativity, and man oh man are the characters alot of fun to watch! I could only imagine how likable Maribel is gonna be, I can imagine how enjoyable the family is gonna be, I can imagine how the other characters outside the family is gonna be, and hell, even though I'm not really a fan of the latin american culture, I was given very pleasant taste of Columbian culture and I'm sure I'm gonna have a big taste of its mythology, and I can't wait to see it. Oh, and guess who's doing the songs for this movie...thats right, my friends, Ol' Lin Manuel Miranda himself and god am I gonna enjoy the songs in this movie, in fact, I bet there might be a favorite song in this movie thats gonna be on my lips for the rest of my life! Its in there, I know it. And lets hope that the songs in this movie will just be as lovable and exciting as the ones from Moana. And as much as I am excited to see this movie is that I really do hope that there will not be a suprise villain in this movie because I absolutely hated those type of villains with a passion and I really do hope that Maribel finds a love interest, of which gender you ask? Eh, anyone, doesn't matter, either boyfriend or girlfriend, love is love. And I really do hope that theres not gonna be a villain at all in this movie and to have the obstacles to be the stand out threat. And last but certainly not least, I really do hope that the ending to this movie will be my favorite ending to a movie of all time! Like an ending thats super emotional but gradually becomes the most happiest sequence you will ever see! In fact I definitely hope that the ending to this movie will be my favorite ending in film in general next to How To Train Your Dragon 3 The Hidden World and Toy Story 4! Overall, Encanto looks like its going to be the best animated feature of the modern era next to Tangled and Moana and also the greatest of this century next to How To Train Your Dragon 3 The Hidden World and Toy Story 4. and maybe Frozen 2. It is the return to making original ideas with original concepts and characters since Moana and lets hope that it will be worth the wait after years of going through Disney sequels (especially with Cars 3). Oh and I also forgot to mention that this will be the return to original animated musicals since Moana. And lets also hope that it will have the same adventurous vibe as Atlantis the lost empire and Tarzan. And last but certainly not least, I really do hope that this movie will be a gigantic box office hit and will have massive positive reviews from critics as well as having 100% at rotten tomatoes and an Oscar for Best Picture. And I gotta say, I absolutely love the direction of which Disney is heading and by watching this whole trailer, I've never been so excited! I've said it before with Moana and I definitely say it with Encanto, this is Disney's way of saying...

I'M BACK BABY!!!! AND BETTER THAN EVER!!!!!

text: No offense, but Disney is taking this diversity thing way too far. I understand they wanna be inclusive and hope no one is offended by what they did in the past, but nobody has been offended and I think it’s only fair to just keep focusing on what you’re into doing until something actually does happen. I say this because as decent as this movie looks, Disney is obviously trying to pay attention to the inclusiveness of female and foreign rights which has no reason to be acknowledged this much since nothing has happened to make you wanna do this. Everything that has happened the past year didn’t necessarily have anything to do with the lack of diversity or inclusiveness and even if it did, that’s a reason to let them decide what they want instead of just assume what they want otherwise you could cause even more problems. In conclusion, I think Disney should stick to paying attention to all cultures and treat them all with the same amount of respect rather than give more respect to one of them as well as stop trying to prevent any situations that haven’t even started yet

text:
Imagine having this as a world in the next _Kingdom Hearts_ game.

I can see this (Encanto) or _Raya and the Last Dragon_ (depending which one Nomura and Disney wound up choosing as a world) be the most recent Disney film and animated film implemented as a world for _Kingdom Hearts IV_ similar to how _Tron: Legacy_ was the most recent Disney film at the time when it was implemented as a world in _Dream Drop Distance_ and when _Big Hero 6_ was included as a world in _Kingdom Hearts III._

However, I remain both skepticsl but also cautiously optimistic that future Disney worlds based on their most recent animated films (Zootopia, Moana, Raya, Encanto) don't go the same route that Arendelle and especially Kingdom of Corona endured in _Kingdom Hearts III_ if they follow the film's plot.

If i'm going to be honest and blunt. I'd say Kingdom of Corona was more frustrating and disappointing than Arendelle in my unpopular opinion mostly during the world's 2nd and 3rd acts due to how Sora, Donald and Goofy stood out like a "sore thumb", removing mentioning of the golden flower, Sora and the gang never questioning Rapunzel's long hair along with witnessing Rapunzel’s magic powers through her hair, changing Flynn/Eugene's character, and especially having Sora and the gang be clueless of Mother Gothel's true colors while remained blissfully unaware about her after their first and *ONLY* time encountering her before Marluxia tolds them through context. Plus, Sora wouldn't shut the hell up when he calls Rapunzel's name which caused him to attract the attention of Mother Gothel qnd later Marluxia. Just watching him calling Rapunzel (the second time before Marluxia appeared) almost made me want to shut him up about to interfere the events of the movie and especially the “world order!” Plus Donald and Goofy did absolutely *NOTHING* and stood idle while Sora remained unconscious thanks to Marluxia until Maximus woke him up the next day. Shame on them.

Even the world itself raised more questions than answers when compared to The Caribbean and Arendelle, which made the world feel forced and linear cause it sure doesn't look like Rapunzel's journey from the tower to the Kingdom was two days like in the film, nor do I think that Rapunzel and Flynn EVER went to the Snuggly Duckling with the Pub Thugs and the Dam when they separated from SDG from the Reaper nobodies, and I don't think that the Guards were also after Flynn as it was only just Maximus that chased him which would explain his dialogue: _"The horse was enough, I don't need monsters on my trail."_ 

On top of that, the scene with Sora, Donald, Goofy and their first and ONLY interaction with Mother Gothel still raised Red Flags, and If I were in their shoes or be right beside them and if Gothel gave me the cold shoulder calling me "useless of no help" I too would raise an eyebrow and think to myself you know something feels of with this woman. Lastly, regardless if the world brought up the "New Seven Hearts" (as Rapunzel is one of them) and Marluxia thematically fit the world (due to the flower motif), neither Rapunzel nor Mother Gothel respectively never brought up his name again for the remainder after their first encounter of him, which made Marluxia's appearance and role in the world very pointless. At least with Monstropolis, Toy Box and San Fransokyo, the inhabitants do acknowledged the Organization XIII member (Young Xehanort, Vanitas, and Dark Riku) and not brush them off like it never happened, particularly the former world when Randall had a "teamup" with Vanitas, even Xaldin was handled much better in Beast's Castle than Marluxia. lol

While I'm not saying Arendelle is better than Corona, that's not the point, but  compared to the other KH3 Disney worlds stories I'd put Corona below The Caribbean, San Fransokyo, Olympus, Monstropolis, and Toy Box. 

Just imagine Sora & Company involved during the climax of the worlds based on Wreck-It Ralph, Moana, Zootopia, Raya and the Last Dragon and/or Encanto but have them be just "there" sticking like a sore thumb in the background not knowing what's going on and doing their own conflict (e.g. Fighting a Giant Heartless), while the movies characters just primarily play out like in the movie but don't acknowledged Sora and the gang until after the conflict is solved.
